phase1.explanation:
🎉 CONGRATULATIONS! 🎉

You've completed ALL 5 units of CS22301 — Object Oriented Programming with Java!

--- Unit 5 Recap ---

1. JavaFX Introduction
   • Application, Stage, Scene
   • JavaFX Application Thread

2. First JavaFX Window
   • Extending Application
   • start() method
   • Stage.show()

3. Layouts
   • VBox, HBox — vertical/horizontal
   • GridPane — grid layout
   • BorderPane — five regions

4. Basic Controls
   • Label, Button, TextField
   • CheckBox, RadioButton
   • ComboBox, ListView

5. Event Handling
   • setOnAction()
   • Property listeners
   • Lambda expressions

6. CSS Styling
   • Inline styles
   • -fx- properties
   • External stylesheets

7. FXML
   • XML UI definition
   • @FXML annotation
   • Controller classes
   • Scene Builder

8. Properties & Binding
   • Observable properties
   • Unidirectional binding
   • Bidirectional binding

9. ListView & TableView
   • ObservableList
   • PropertyValueFactory
   • Cell factories

10. Dialogs & Alerts
    • Alert types
    • Custom dialogs
    • Input dialogs

11. Animation
    • Transitions (Translate, Fade, Rotate, Scale)
    • Timeline
    • KeyFrame

12. Mini App
    • To-Do List
    • Calculator
    • Contact Manager

--- The Complete Java Journey ---

Unit 1: Java Basics
• Syntax, variables, loops, arrays, methods, Strings

Unit 2: OOP Deep Dive
• Inheritance, polymorphism, abstract classes, interfaces
• Encapsulation, packages, static, final

Unit 3: Exception Handling & I/O
• try-catch, custom exceptions, streams
• File I/O, serialization, NIO, CSV

Unit 4: Collections & Threads
• ArrayList, HashMap, HashSet
• Generics, Comparable, Comparator
• Multithreading, synchronization, executors

Unit 5: JavaFX GUI
• All the UI goodness!

--- What's Next? ---

🚀 Spring Framework:
• Build web applications
• Dependency injection
• REST APIs
• Spring Boot

📱 Android Development:
• Mobile apps with Java/Kotlin
• Android Studio
• Material Design

🏢 Enterprise Java:
• Jakarta EE
• Microservices
• JPA (database)
• JMS (messaging)

📊 Data Science:
• Apache Spark
• Data processing
• Machine learning

--- Java Ecosystem Map ---

Core Java ← You are here!
    ↓
    ├── Web: Spring, Jakarta EE, Servlets
    ├── Mobile: Android
    ├── Desktop: JavaFX, Swing
    ├── Big Data: Hadoop, Spark
    ├── Cloud: AWS, Azure, GCP
    └── Microservices: Spring Boot, Micronaut

--- Java-chan's Final Words ---

You've come so far! From writing your first Hello World to building full GUI applications with JavaFX.

Remember:
• Keep practicing
• Build projects
• Read others' code
• Stay curious

Java is a powerful language with endless possibilities. You've built a solid foundation to explore all of them.

Happy coding! ☕💻

--- Course Complete! ---

⭐ You've earned 75 lessons of XP!
⭐ You're now ready for professional Java development!
⭐ Go build something amazing!

phase2.explanation:
Conceptual review — no broken code!

You've completed the entire course! Here are some final tips:

1. Keep Learning:
   • Build projects
   • Contribute to open source
   • Read documentation

2. Practice Regularly:
   • Code every day
   • Solve coding challenges
   • Review previous lessons

3. Stay Updated:
   • Java releases every 6 months
   • New features (records, pattern matching, etc.)
   • Follow Java community

4. Next Steps:
   • Choose a specialization
   • Build a portfolio
   • Apply for jobs

You've got this! 💪

phase3.prompt:
What is the recommended framework for building web applications with Java?

A) JavaFX
B) Swing
C) Spring Framework
D) Android SDK

Select the CORRECT answer.
dialogueHints: Think about what's used for web development...
JavaFX and Swing are for desktop.
phase3.openingDialogue: One final question! What's next in your Java journey? 🤔
